from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about astronauts (or related entities) into a
    single fluent sentence. Uses predicate‑specific phrasing when possible
    and combines all clauses into one natural‑language sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language templates.
    # The placeholder {subject} and {object} will be substituted.
    templates = {
        "almaMater": "{subject} studied at {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
        "birthName": "{subject}'s birth name was {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "dateOfRetirement": "{subject} retired in {object}",
        "occupation": "{subject} worked as a {object}",
        "status": "{subject} is {object}",
        "timeInSpace": "{subject} spent {object} minutes in space",
        "mission": "{subject} flew on the mission {object}",
        "selectedByNasa": "{subject} was selected by NASA in {object}",
        "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} was a citizen of {object}",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "{subject} served as chief of the astronaut office in {object}",
        "title": "{subject} held the title of {object}",
        "ribbonAward": "{subject} received the ribbon award {object}",
        "operator": "{subject} was operated by {object}",
        "backupPilot": "{subject} had {object} as backup pilot",
        "commander": "{subject} was commanded by {object}",
        "crewMembers": "{subject} had crew member {object}",
        "representative": "{subject} was represented by {object}",
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "awards": "{subject} received {object} awards",
        "award": "{subject} received the award {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "fossil": "{subject} is known for the fossil {object}",
        "senators": "{subject} is represented by senator {object}",
        "part": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "bird": "{subject}'s state bird is {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "affiliation": "{subject} is affiliated with {object}",
        "competeIn": "{subject} competes in {object}",
        "president": "{subject}'s president is {object}",
        "award": "{subject} received the award {object}",
        "gemstone": "{subject}'s state gemstone is {object}",
        "mascot": "{subject}'s mascot is {object}",
        "cosparId": "{subject} has COSPAR ID {object}",
        "utcOffset": "{subject} has UTC offset {object}",
        # Default fallback will be added later.
    }

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects.
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        obj = obj.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            return obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Helper templates without the subject placeholder.
    templates_no_subj = {
        "almaMater": "studied at {object}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {object}",
        "birthName": "{subject}'s birth name was {object}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {object}",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired in {object}",
        "occupation": "worked as a {object}",
        "status": "is {object}",
        "timeInSpace": "spent {object} minutes in space",
        "mission": "flew on the mission {object}",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in {object}",
        "deathDate": "died on {object}",
        "nationality": "was a citizen of {object}",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as chief of the astronaut office in {object}",
        "title": "held the title of {object}",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon award {object}",
        "operator": "was operated by {object}",
        "backupPilot": "had {object} as backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by {object}",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member {object}",
        "representative": "was represented by {object}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {object}",
        "awards": "received {object} awards",
        "award": "received the award {object}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {object}",
        "fossil": "is known for the fossil {object}",
        "senators": "is represented by senator {object}",
        "part": "is part of {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "bird": "state bird is {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with {object}",
        "competeIn": "competes in {object}",
        "president": "president is {object}",
        "gemstone": "state gemstone is {object}",
        "mascot": "mascot is {object}",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID {object}",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset {object}",
    }

    def _make_clause(t):
        tmpl = templates_no_subj.get(t.predicate)
        obj_clean = _clean(t.object)
        if tmpl:
            # For the rare case where the template still needs the subject (e.g., birthName)
            try:
                return tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=obj_clean)
            except KeyError:
                return tmpl.format(object=obj_clean)
        else:
            pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
            return f"{pred} {obj_clean}"

    subject_phrases = []
    for subj, triples_list in subject_groups.items():
        # Build clauses for this subject without repeating the subject.
        sub_clauses = [_make_clause(t) for t in triples_list]
        if len(sub_clauses) == 1:
            phrase = f"{subj} {sub_clauses[0]}"
        else:
            phrase = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(sub_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + sub_clauses[-1]
        subject_phrases.append(phrase)

    # Combine different subject phrases into a single sentence using semicolons.
    sentence = "; ".join(subject_phrases)

    # Capitalize first character and ensure final period.
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
